zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Thornbury於1375年出生在英國瓦維其可爾卻斯特頓時，她的父親Sir John de Thornbury26歲，母親Naverina de Knightley21歲。她於1390年與Sir John Knightley在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾格諾薩爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1418年10月3日享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾卻斯特頓聖 吉盧也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Thornbury于1375年出生在英国瓦維其可爾卻斯特頓时，她的父亲Sir John de Thornbury26岁，母亲Naverina de Knightley21岁。她于1390年与Sir John Knightley在英国英格兰斯塔福爾格諾薩爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1418年10月3日享年43岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾卻斯特頓聖 吉盧也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thornbury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’; compare   Thornton  .

variants.name: Throneberry
meanings: Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of German   Throneburg  .

variants.name: Thornberry
meanings: English (southwestern): habitational name from any of various places called Thornbury, in particular Thornbury (Gloucestershire), Thornbury (Herefordshire), or perhaps Thornbury (Devon), or Thornbury in Bradford (Yorkshire). These placenames derive from Old English thorn ‘thorn’ + burg ‘fortress’ or ‘manor’.
Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’; compare   Thornton  .

variants.name: Thornburgh
meanings: English (Worcestershire) and Irish: variant of   Thornbury  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Thornbury was born in 1375, in Chesterton, Warwickshire, England, her father, Sir John de Thornbury, was 26 and her mother, Naverina de Knightley, was 21. She married Sir John Knightley in 1390, in Gnosall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 3 October 1418, at the age of 43, and was buried in St Giles' Church, Chesterton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Sir John Knightley en 1390, à Gnosall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 octobre 1418, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à St Giles' Church, Chesterton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Thornburyが1375年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアチェステルトンで生まれた時，父Sir John de Thornburyは26歳，母Naverina de Knightleyは21歳であった。1390年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアグノサルでSir John Knightleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1418年10月3日に43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアチェステルトンスト ギレス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Giles Church - Chesterton, Chesterto: 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 체스터턴 스트 길스' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Thornbury(이)가 1375년 영국 워윅실르 체스터턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Thornbury(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Naverina de Knightley(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 뇰사에서 Sir John Knightley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1418년 10월 3일 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 체스터턴 스트 길스' c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.St. Giles Church - Chesterton, Chesterto: ст джилес' чурч, честиртон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Thornbury родилась в 1375 г. в местности честиртон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Thornbury было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Naverina de Knightley, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Knightley в 1390 г. в местности гносалл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 3 октября 1418 г. в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности ст джилес' чурч, честиртон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Sir John Knightley en 1390, en Gnosall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 3 de octubre de 1418, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultada en St Giles' Church, Chesterton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
